tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Having tested weaponized nuclear warheads, Operation Shakti achieved India's goal of becoming a declared nuclear-weapon state, establishing a credible minimum deterrent, and demonstrating indigenous technical capability.
The tests enabled India to avoid reliance on foreign technology for security. Our heartfelt gratitude goes out to the entire collective of brilliant minds - the scientists, engineers, technicians, and military personnel - who worked to make the Pokhran-II tests a reality.
tweets.content:
Having tested weaponized nuclear warheads, Operation Shakti achieved India's goal of becoming a declared nuclear-weapon state, establishing a credible minimum deterrent, and demonstrating indigenous technical capability.
The tests enabled India to avoid reliance on foreign technology for security. Our heartfelt gratitude goes out to the entire collective of brilliant minds - the scientists, engineers, technicians, and military personnel - who worked to make the Pokhran-II tests a reality.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: The first test was planned on 11 May. The thermonuclear device was placed in a shaft code named White House, which was approximately 230m deep, the fission bomb was placed in a 150m deep shaft code named Taj Mahal, and the first sub-kiloton device in shaft Kumbhkaran.
The timing of the tests was pursuant to the local weather conditions, and the test sequence was initiated in the afternoon.

The timing of the tests was pursuant to the local weather conditions, and the test sequence was initiated in the afternoon.

R to @NetramDefence: The bombs were transported from BARC at 3 am on 1 May 1998 to Mumbai airport, then flown in an Indian Air Force's AN-32 aircraft to Jaisalmer Airport.
It was then transported to Pokhran in an army convoy of trucks, and this required numerous trips.
The devices were delivered to the device preparation building, which was designated as Prayer Hall.

The bombs were transported from BARC at 3 am on 1 May 1998 to Mumbai airport, then flown in an Indian Air Force's AN-32 aircraft to Jaisalmer Airport.
It was then transported to Pokhran in an army convoy of trucks, and this required numerous trips.
The devices were delivered to the device preparation building, which was designated as Prayer Hall.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: India's Intelligence Bureau had been fully aware of the capability of the United States spy satellites in detecting Indian test preparations.
Therefore, the tests required complete secrecy. The 58th Engineer Regiment of the Indian Army Corps of Engineers was tasked with preparing the test sites without being detected.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Pokhran-II was a series of five nuclear weapon tests conducted by India in May 1998. The bombs were detonated at the Indian Army's Pokhran Test Range in Rajasthan.
It was the second instance of nuclear testing conducted by India, after the first test, Smiling Buddha, in May 1974.

tweets.content:
THREAD 🧵
National Technology Day | Pokhran-II Nuclear Tests

National Technology Day is celebrated in India annually on May 11 to commemorate the successful Pokhran-II nuclear tests ("Operation Shakti") of 1998, marking a major milestone in indigenous technology.
It honors scientists and engineers, with the 2026 theme focusing on "Responsible Innovation for Inclusive Growth".

- A seasoned mid-level scrap collector can earn up to ₹10K–₹12K per day. The game is to build a network of other waste collectors who can supply good-quality scrap.

- The industry is booming, and businesses in it are extremely cash-rich. You’re looking at millionaire-level annual numbers here.

I have always had a theory in my mind: are there groups across countries motivated to damage infrastructure and steal public assets meant for citizens’ safety and convenience?

We can't kill the snake, but we can defang it to get 10-15 years of uninterrupted focus on national growth.

We aren’t celebrating this enough..

Understand: At Mach 6+, you aren't building a square pipe. You’re managing a 2,500C inferno for 20 minutes. 

This is a total thermal soak that would melt any standard structures (including ones used in Brahmos)

The Complexity:
✅ Material Science : A hybrid of C-C composites, SiC/SiC liners, and nickel superalloys.
✅ Physics: Managing supersonic shockwaves while the engine's geometry literally expands from the heat.
✅ Most importantly Manufacturing: Mastering precision diffusion bonding and superalloys indigenously is the real "world beater" with our own industrial base here.

• Long-duration hypersonic combustion
• Active cooling architecture
• Indigenous endothermic fuel
• Advanced thermal barrier coatings
A critical step toward India’s future Hypersonic Cruise Missile program(HCM).
